stories.headline: Iran war live: Ceasefire starts in Lebanon as Trump says Tehran deal close
stories.summary: Death toll from Israeli attacks on Lebanon reached 2,196 on Thursday as 10-day ceasefire announced.

stories.headline: Celebrations in Lebanon as 10-day ceasefire with Israel begins
stories.summary: Celebratory gunfire heard in Beirut as truce takes effect, though Lebanese military reports violations.

stories.headline: ‘Trump forced Israel into a ceasefire’ with Lebanon
stories.summary: Abed Abou Shhadeh, a political commentator in Israel, says the ceasefire between Israel and Lebanon is ‘problematic'.

stories.headline: US-brokered 10-day ceasefire between Israel and Lebanon goes into effect
stories.summary: Trump extended an invitation to Lebanese President Joseph Aoun and Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu to hold the first direct meeting at that level in decades.

stories.headline: Australia's most decorated living soldier granted bail over war crime charges
stories.summary: Lawyers argued that Ben Roberts-Smith wouldn't be able to defend himself properly from prison.

stories.headline: Harry and Meghan meet Bondi shooting survivors
stories.summary: On the fourth day of their Australian visit, the royal couple pay tribute to the 15 people killed in the Bondi shooting.

stories.headline: Trump Says Iran Deal ‘Looking Very Good’ Amid Ceasefire
stories.summary: President Donald Trump says the US and Iran could clinch a permanent ceasefire, with talks between Washington and Tehran possibly resuming this weekend. Bloomberg's Laura Davison breaks down the latest. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: Wall Street Turns Gloomy on the Dollar as Haven Demand Fades
stories.summary: Deutsche Bank AG and Wells Fargo & Co. are among banks declaring the dollar’s war-driven haven rally is likely over as the fragile ceasefire between the US and Iran prompts investors to seek riskier assets.

Major refinery fire won't lead to fuel rationing, Australian PM says
stories.summary: A 13-hour blaze at one of Australia's two oil refineries could impact petrol prices and supply.

stories.headline: China Moves to Slow Yuan Rally by Cooling Fixing Volatility
stories.summary: China’s central bank is using its daily reference rate to temper the yuan’s rally as the currency’s outperformance during the Iran war fuels further bullish sentiment.

stories.headline: China to Commission Seven Nuclear Reactors in 2026, CCTV Says
stories.summary: Seven nuclear power reactors are scheduled to be completed and commissioned in China this year, state broadcaster China Central Television reported, citing the nation’s atomic energy organization’s annual report.

stories.headline: Pemex Undersea Pipeline Found to Cause Gulf Oil Spill
stories.summary: An oil spill in the Gulf of Mexico that soiled beaches, killed wildlife and sparked outrage from environmental groups was caused by a leaky pipeline owned by state oil company Petroleos Mexicanos, the company said.

stories.headline: Acting ICE Director Says He Plans to Resign in May
stories.summary: Todd Lyons said he would leave to spend more time with his family. He has spoken about a surge in threats against ICE officers, saying that he knew the reality firsthand.

stories.headline: Trump administration urges US oil bosses to increase drilling
stories.summary: Energy and interior secretaries hold call with executives to encourage more production

stories.headline: Oaktree’s Senior Credit Executive for Asia Set to Depart Firm
stories.summary: One of Oaktree Capital Group LLC’s most senior investment professionals for Asia is set to leave the firm in the coming months, according to people familiar with the matter.

stories.headline: What’s Next for Manycore After Blockbuster HK Debut
stories.summary: Hangzhou-based Manycore Tech, one of the so-called "Six Little Dragons" of Chinese AI startups, surged as much as 187% during its trading debut in Hong Kong. Its co-founder and chairman Victor Huang, who previously worked at Nvidia, says Manycore plans to integrate new technologies and expand into new markets. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: Indian Stocks Risk Deeper Underperformance as Tech-Led North Asia Draws Funds
stories.summary: Market’s appeal among Asia money managers has eroded.

stories.headline: OCBC Is Said to Emerge as Lead Bidder for HSBC Indonesia Assets
stories.summary: Oversea-Chinese Banking Corp. has emerged as the preferred bidder to buy HSBC Holdings Plc’s retail assets in Indonesia, people familiar with the matter said, as the Singaporean lender seeks to boost its presence in Southeast Asia’s largest economy.

stories.headline: Investors Should 'Look Under the Hood' of Markets: IG International
stories.summary: IG International's Fabien Yip says that while the stock market "is striking new highs," it's important for investors to "look under the hood" as some corporate earnings and news "could potentially give a shock to the rally that we're seeing." (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: UBS’ Senior Indonesia Wealth Banker Leaves Amid Several Exits
stories.summary: UBS Group AG’s senior wealth banker Lionel Yoong has left the Swiss firm, adding to several recent departures from the group covering Indonesia from Singapore.

stories.headline: Credit Growth to Lift India Bank Profits Despite Likely FX Losses
stories.summary: Strong credit growth should have kept fourth-quarter numbers healthy at Indian banks including HDFC Bank Ltd. and ICICI Bank Ltd., though earnings may have been dented by trading losses linked to the central bank’s rupee derivatives curbs.

stories.headline: Medical Device Maker Alamar Bio Boosts IPO to Raise $191 Million
stories.summary: Alamar Biosciences Inc., a maker of medical devices that detect protein biomarkers to diagnose diseases, raised $191 million in an upsized US initial public offering that priced at the top of a marketed range.

stories.headline: IMF, World Bank say they are restoring ties with Venezuela
stories.summary: Acting President Delcy Rodriguez calls resumption of ties 'great achievement' of Venezuelan diplomacy.

People in Beirut wary of trusting Israel will uphold Lebanon ceasefire
stories.summary: Lebanon’s residents say they are wary of trusting that Israel will abide by the ceasefire agreement announced by Trump.

stories.headline: 10-day ceasefire in Lebanon begins as Israel agrees to U.S.-backed deal
stories.summary: President Donald Trump announced the agreement, which went into force Thursday evening, as Pakistani mediators worked to extend a U.S.-Iran ceasefire and arrange new talks.

stories.headline: Israel and Lebanon’s 10-day ceasefire goes into effect
stories.summary: A 10-day ceasefire between Israel and Lebanon has gone into effect. It began at midnight local time (21:00 GMT).

stories.headline: Displaced Lebanese wary as ceasefire between Israel and Hezbollah begins
stories.summary: Tensions linger as evacuees await clarity on the ceasefire’s terms before risking a return to war-torn villages.

stories.headline: Israel and Lebanon agree to 10-day ceasefire, Trump says in social media post
stories.summary: Trump had announced earlier on Thursday that the leaders of Israel and Lebanon would speak about halting the fighting, in what would have been the first direct conversation in more than 30 years.

stories.headline: Russia ‘does not deserve’ lifting of sanctions, Zelenskyy says, after deadly overnight strikes in Ukraine – as it happened
stories.summary: Ukrainian president says nearly 700 Russian drones and 19 ballistic missiles mostly targeted Kyiv, Odesa and DniproGerman chancellor Friedrich Merz and Irish prime minister Micheál Martin are now speaking at a press conference after their meeting in Berlin.Let’s listen in. stories.headline: Potential targets: Moscow releases data about European firms making drones for Ukraine
stories.summary: Since the beginning of Russia's full-scale invasion of Ukraine, European countries have provided Kyiv with extensive financial and military aid, supplying the armed forces with weapons systems and ammunition.

stories.headline: Oil Drops as Trump Strikes Upbeat Tone With War Near 50-Day Mark
stories.summary: Oil dropped as President Donald Trump struck an optimistic tone on prospects for a permanent ceasefire between the US and Iran.

stories.headline: Trump Says Deal With Iran ‘Looking Very Good’ Amid Ceasefire
stories.summary: President Donald Trump struck an optimistic tone for prospects that the US and Iran could clinch a permanent ceasefire as the two sides discuss an extended truce ahead of its expiration next week.

stories.headline: Henry Paulson Suggests US Make a Break-Glass Treasuries Plan
stories.summary: Former Treasury Secretary Henry Paulson suggested US authorities prepare a back-up plan in order to avert a potential future collapse in demand for Treasuries resulting from long-running concerns over the federal debt load — an event that he warned would have “vicious” effects.

stories.headline: House Votes to Preserve Deportation Protections for Haitians, Rebuking Trump
stories.summary: The action was largely symbolic since the president would be all but certain to veto the bill, but the bipartisan vote reflected resistance within his own ranks on his signature issue.

stories.headline: Trump says Iran agrees to hand over ‘nuclear dust’
stories.summary: Iran has not confirmed Trump’s claim. Giving up its highly enriched uranium would be a major step toward an agreement.

stories.headline: House votes to restore protections for Haitians, defying Trump
stories.summary: Ten House Republicans joined Democrats to oppose President Donald Trump on his immigration policy Thursday, voting to restore temporary protections for Haitians living in the United States.

stories.headline: Armed robbers hold 25 people hostage at Naples bank before fleeing through hole in floor
stories.summary: Thieves believed to have escaped into sewers after holding staff and customers in Crédit Agricole branch for two hoursArmed robbers held 25 people hostage at a bank in Naples for two hours on Thursday, before fleeing through a tunnel.The three thieves entered a branch of Crédit Agricole in the southern Italian city at about 11.30am, taking hostage staff and customers, who were freed by police a couple of hours later. stories.headline: US House votes to extend temporary protections for Haitians in Trump rebuke
stories.summary: Ten Republicans join Democrats to extend Temporary Protected Status for nearly 350,000 Haitians in the United States.

stories.headline: AOC, Boebert join calls for sexual misconduct accountability in Congress
stories.summary: Lawmakers who represent  leftward and rightward flanks of their parties call for reform after Congress members resign.

stories.headline: Is it worth stockpiling cash as phsyical currency makes comeback in Poland?
stories.summary: While the trend towards the complete digitalisation of payments prevailed not long ago, today central banks and governments say that physical money may prove indispensable in crisis situations.

stories.headline: Pope criticises 'tyrants' who spend billions on wars after Trump spat
stories.summary: The comments follow a high-profile spat with US President Donald Trump, who called the Pope weak on crime.

stories.headline: Turkish police detain 162 people over online posts about school shootings
stories.summary: At least 16 people were injured in a shooting at a high school on Tuesday, before another nine were killed in a separate school shooting on Wednesday.

stories.headline: Naples bank robbers hold 25 people hostage then vanish through tunnel
stories.summary: The armed men reportedly evaded capture by escaping through the city's sewer system.

stories.headline: Russia launches deadliest aerial attack in months, killing 18 in Ukraine
stories.summary: A Ukrainian drone attack killed two people in Russia, Moscow says, after an Orthodox Easter truce.

stories.headline: Europe has 'maybe six weeks of jet fuel left', energy boss warns
stories.summary: Flights could soon be cancelled if supplies from the Gulf remain blocked, says the International Energy Agency.

stories.headline: Japan pledges $10bn to help Asian countries deal with oil crisis
stories.summary: The aid is roughly equivalent to a year's worth of crude oil imports by Asean countries.

stories.headline: The great independence charade
stories.summary: Scottish and Welsh election results may reignite the debate but it is all one big pantomime

stories.headline: Stock Rally Pauses as Traders Await Iran Ceasefire: Markets Wrap
stories.summary: A record-breaking rally in global equities stalled in Asia as investors trimmed positions ahead of the weekend while awaiting progress on extending the US-Iran ceasefire.

stories.headline: Judge Again Halts Aboveground Construction on Trump Ballroom
stories.summary: A federal judge wrote that an exception he made for work on security features did not cover most of the construction on the larger ballroom project Trump has proposed.

stories.headline: Federal judge sets new limits on Trump ballroom construction
stories.summary: U.S. District Judge Richard Leon clarified that some national security work can continue while the overall project is halted. Trump attacked him on social media.

stories.headline: How South Korea plans to use the Iran crisis to spur a renewables revolution
stories.summary: Energy crisis unfolding in Middle East has added political urgency, and more funding, to transform South Korea’s solar industryIn Guyang-ri, a farming village of 70 households about 90 minutes south-east of Seoul, people gather for communal free lunches six days a week. stories.headline: South African opposition figure Malema sentenced to five years in prison for firing a gun
stories.summary: Malema is appealing against the decision, which means he is not going to prison immediately.

stories.headline: 'Unprecedented' fire at Australian oil refinery to affect nation's petrol supplies
stories.summary: The fire has deepened fears over the nation's petrol supplies amid a global crunch.

stories.headline: India pushes for women's quota bill as row over parliamentary seats intensifies
stories.summary: Southern Indian leaders urge mass mobilisation over concerns about redrawing electoral boundaries.

stories.headline: 'How does one survive?': Factory protests expose strain in India's industrial system
stories.summary: Workers in some north Indian cities have been protesting, demanding better pay and working conditions.

stories.summary: Discussions come despite federal lawsuits over whether the start-up is a national security risk

stories.headline: Trump adviser Sebastian Gorka seeks top counterterrorism job
stories.summary: The maneuvering comes a month after Joe Kent, the National Counterterrorism Center’s previous director, resigned in protest over the Iran war.

stories.headline: Nine killed in second Turkish school shooting in two days
stories.summary: Eight students and one teacher died in the attack, according to Interior Minister Mustafa Cifci.

stories.headline: Trump hints Iran talks could resume this week as US port blockade continues
stories.summary: The United Nations' secretary general said it was "highly probable" that the negotiations would restart.

stories.headline: David Zaslav and the tyranny of incentives

stories.headline: Magyar calls for fast handover of power in talks with Hungary's president
stories.summary: Péter Magyar, the man who ended Viktor Orbán's 16 years of continuous rule, wants the new parliament to convene in early May.

stories.headline: Spain approves plan to give around 500,000 undocumented migrants legal status
stories.summary: Prime Minister Pedro Sánchez said migrants helped "build the rich, open and diverse Spain that we are and to which we aspire".

stories.headline: Bongbong Marcos fights ill-health rumours with star jumps
stories.summary: The Philippine President challenged anyone questioning his fitness to join him in the gym.

stories.headline: America’s corporate boards are under siege
stories.summary: Activist shareholders and regulatory changes are undermining their control

stories.headline: Italian PM condemns ally Trump over 'unacceptable' Pope criticism
stories.summary: Italy's prime minister and the US president are close allies, but Trump has refused to apologise to the "very weak" Pope Leo XIV.

stories.headline: UK could adopt EU single market rules under new legislation
stories.summary: The move has raised questions over parliamentary scrutiny of future rules to deliver planned EU deals.

stories.headline: Orbán era swept away by Péter Magyar's Hungary election landslide
stories.summary: Viktor Orbán's 16-year rule is over, defeated by a 45-year-old ex-party insider who convinced a majority of Hungarians to oust him.

stories.headline: Peru election drags into second day after ballot delivery fiasco
stories.summary: Technical and logistical problems mean Peruvians choosing a new president and members of Congress continue to vote on Monday.

stories.headline: Ukraine and Russia accuse each other of hundreds of ceasefire violations
stories.summary: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky says his nation's forces would respond "symmetrically" to Russian attacks.
